gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Guarantee tag on the honesty lattice `Exact ⊐ Proven ⊐ Empirical ⊐ Declared`"] # [doc = " (RFC-0016 §4.1 C2; VR-5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " All values are `'static` strings matching the lattice names so they can be"] # [doc = " asserted in tests without a dependency on the core lattice type from this"] # [doc = " matrix module."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum GuaranteeTag { # [doc = " No accuracy / precision / probability semantics; the operation is the"] # [doc = " honest floor `Exact` (RFC-0016 C2)."] Exact , # [doc = " The property is established by a **checked side-condition theorem** (VR-5)."] # [doc = " Not used in this matrix — no round-trip theorem has been checked."] Proven , # [doc = " The property holds over a **generated corpus** (proptest); not `Proven`"] # [doc = " because no theorem with checked side-conditions exists (VR-5)."] Empirical , # [doc = " The property is **asserted without a checked basis**; always FLAGGED (VR-5)."] Declared , }
gaps.reason: enum variant/constructor `Exact` collides with a Mycelium reserved word — emitting it verbatim would fail to parse (the lexer tokenizes it as a keyword, not an identifier); no sanctioned auto-rename in this PoC, so flagged rather than emitted (G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet: impl GuaranteeTag { # [doc = " Human-readable name matching the lattice notation (`\"Exact\"`, etc.)."] # [must_use] pub fn as_str (self) -> & 'static str { match self { GuaranteeTag :: Exact => "Exact" , GuaranteeTag :: Proven => "Proven" , GuaranteeTag :: Empirical => "Empirical" , GuaranteeTag :: Declared => "Declared" , } } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (1 sub-issue(s)): impl method `as_str` signature: `String`/`str` — `Bytes` exists in base_type but is not confirmed equivalent to a UTF-8 text type

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Fallibility classification for an exported op (C1)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Fallibility { # [doc = " The op cannot fail for any well-formed input."] Total , # [doc = " The op returns an explicit `Result` or `Option`; the error set is named in"] # [doc = " `error_set`."] Fallible , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Whether the op surfaces an EXPLAIN artifact (C3)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Explainable { # [doc = " The op carries an RFC-0013 diagnostic record with the failure locus —"] # [doc = " the machine-legible EXPLAIN surface."] Yes , # [doc = " The op neither selects, converts, nor approximates; there is no hidden"] # [doc = " decision to expose (C3 `n/a`)."] NotApplicable , }
